title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Independent Container Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer buildx builders, multi-platform images, bake, and oci outputs into a new multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Buildx Builders Drivers
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Buildx Builders Drivers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, keep Docker Buildx Builders Drivers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Bake Matrix Outputs
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-bake-matrix-outputs-erjo3w". Tailor its docker-bake.hcl change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Bake Matrix Outputs.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Bake Matrix Outputs into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Bake Matrix Outputs, keep Docker Bake Matrix Outputs connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-bake-matrix-outputs-erjo3w
  - competency: docker-bake-matrix-outputs
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: bake matrix outputs
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: remove dependency readiness while preserving start order
    retained-proof: redacted configuration render and secret-rotation confirmation
    case-key: erjo3w
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Bake Matrix Outputs, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A lab release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Bake Matrix Outputs, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: docker buildx bake always builds one target and loads it into the local image store.
